From an extremely left-wing perspective on both economic and personal-freedom grounds, military action that violates international law is never acceptable. International law is one of the few global mechanisms that can restrain the aggressive use of state power (especially by powerful capitalist states) and protect the sovereignty and self-determination of peoples. Breaking it undermines collective security, invites imperialist adventures, and inevitably curtails the personal freedoms and lives of those on the receiving end of such violence.

Compulsory attendance is a coercive, top-down imposition on young people’s personal freedom. A left-wing stance on personal freedom demands that individuals—even children—have the right to decide how, when, and whether they engage with formal education. Removing compulsion respects bodily and intellectual autonomy, undermines authoritarian schooling structures, and opens space for more liberatory, community-controlled learning models.
Answer: 1 – strongly disagree.

Segregationist or “keep to their own kind” thinking is fundamentally incompatible with both left-wing economic values (which emphasize collective solidarity across groups) and left-wing personal-freedom values (which insist on the right of every individual to associate, love, and live as they choose without imposed boundaries).
Answer: 1 – strongly disagree.

From an extremely left-wing perspective on personal freedom, bodily autonomy and non-violence are paramount. Children are full persons whose physical integrity must be respected; hitting them is an infringement of that autonomy and an abuse of power. Parental authority does not include the right to use violence, and society should actively discourage corporal punishment rather than normalize it.
4 – Strongly agree.
